Scheduling analysis of 1 program across 4 terms of schedule data.

PROGRAMS
- Biology AS-T — needs fix: full-time 4 terms (needs schedule change); part-time 6 terms.
    published map broken: BIOLOGY 3 mapped to term 1 but only offered Fall
    recommended fix: add PHYSICS 6 in Spring

SUPPLY BOTTLENECKS (course-level, across all programs)
- Rotation gaps (required course offered in too few terms): PHYSICS 6 (1/4)
- Single-section risk (only one section — a single point of failure): BIOLOGY 3
- Under-supply (sections at capacity / waitlist pressure): MATH 227 (2/3 sections waitlisted)
BUILD: campus LAMC · terms 2268, 2272 · program Biology AS-T (AS-T)

TERM-BY-TERM PLANS
- Biology AS-T [Full-time]: T1: BIOLOGY 3, CHEM 101 | T2: MATH 261
- Biology AS-T [Part-time]: T1: BIOLOGY 3 | T2: CHEM 101

GENERAL EDUCATION
Pattern igetc (ASSIST status: ok).
- Area 1A English Composition: need 1, plan concrete
- Area 2A Mathematical Concepts: need 1, plan shared
Met by major: BIOLOGY 3, MATH 261

LIVE RECONCILIATION
6 program courses offered in the fetched terms, 2 not offered. Not offered: PHYSICS 6, GEOG 15.

TIME CONFLICTS (required courses that clash by meeting time)
- CHEM 101 & MATH 245 — every offered section overlaps

PROGRAM BUILDABILITY (structural-feasibility PROXY, NOT a measured completion rate)
- Biology AS-T (score 47/100 GE-inclusive; major-only 55, Δ -8): 3/4 required offered; missing PHYSICS 6; time-conflict-free; 1 single-section; GE 1/2 areas schedulable, gaps 4 [DRAFT GE].

(+5 more add-a-section course(s) not shown.)
Capacity slack (review only — NOT a cut recommendation): ART 101 (fill 0.14).
(1 required course(s) had no seat counts — excluded, not silently counted.)

GRID CONFORMANCE & MORNING COMPRESSION (structural PROXY, NOT a measured completion rate)
- on-grid start times: 90%; prime 9AM-1PM share: 0.7; morning-locked required courses: 2.
- mutually exclusive (both morning-locked): A0 & B0.
- mutually exclusive (both morning-locked): A1 & B1.
- mutually exclusive (both morning-locked): A2 & B2.

- mutually exclusive (both morning-locked): A5 & B5.
(+5 more mutually-exclusive pair(s) not shown.)

EQUITY / ARCHETYPE EXPOSURE (structural exposure PROXY, NOT a measured equity outcome)
- Evening-only (start ≥ 5:00 PM) (kept 1/3 sections): 1 program(s) collapse.
  - Biology AS-T: score 48, Δ -23; loses CHEM 101, MATH 261.
- Online-only: not assessed (section modality (classType) is not present on the imported records).
- Two days a week (≤ 2 meeting days) (kept 2/3 sections): 0 program(s) collapse.
(+2 more newly-unavailable required course(s) not shown.)

WHY A PLAN IS INFEASIBLE (a deterministic STRUCTURAL diagnostic of the minimal required-course set the planner cannot schedule in a cohort's term horizon; NOT a student outcome — season mismatches are excluded as fixable, and GE is held as fixed background)
- Biology AS-T (Full-time, 4 terms): these 2 required course(s) cannot all be scheduled within the 4-term full-time plan; relaxing any one restores feasibility [MATH 261, CHEM 101].

FIRST-YEAR GATEWAY MOMENTUM (offering PROXY for whether the transfer-level English/Math gateway can be SCHEDULED in year 1, NOT a measured completion rate; a major-subject-fallback gateway is discipline-level, transfer-level UNVERIFIED)
- English: ENGL 101 (via ge_area_1A, transfer-level area-defined) — schedulable in year 1.
- Math: MATH 227 (via major_subject, transfer-level unverified) — NOT schedulable in year 1; not offered in the analyzed schedule.
  First-year window: 2268, 2272. Both gateways schedulable: no.

COREQUISITE CO-AVAILABILITY (AB1705 co-OFFERING STRUCTURE PROXY for whether the gateway's catalog corequisite runs in the SAME first-year term; NOT a measured or causal outcome — per AB1705, DIRECT PLACEMENT was the dominant lever and corequisite is one supported form)
- English: ENGL 101 (corequisite ENGL 101L) — co-offered in year 1.
- Math: MATH 150 (corequisite MATH 150L) — NOT co-offered in year 1; corequisite MATH 150L is not offered in the analyzed schedule.

COURSE SUCCESS SIGNAL (MEASURED aggregate retention/success from a CCCCO Data Mart export, Course granularity; NOT a completion or student-level outcome and NOT this schedule's outcome — a low rate next to a supply constraint is a co-occurrence, not causal)
- ESCALATED CHEM 101: success 40% (also supply-constrained).
- MATH 261: success 55%.

WHY THIS MATTERS — SECTOR-WIDE RESEARCH EVIDENCE (published findings from OTHER institutions explaining why a flagged condition matters; NOT a measurement or prediction of this campus)
- 30%→43%; 9%→~18%; 23.7%→34.8%: Guided-pathway course maps with structured, data-informed advising raised graduation rates: Central Arizona College 30%→43%, Community College of Philadelphia 9%→~18% (doubled), and Bluegrass / BCTC 23.7%→34.8%. [Source: Central Arizona College, Community College of Philadelphia, Bluegrass Community & Technical College; reported via Community College Daily and CCRC]
- 57% of students affected: 57% of students report having to spend more time and money because required courses are not offered at the times they need them. [Source: Student-survey finding aggregated in the completion-research review (ScienceDirect / Community College Daily source set)]
- +2.3–2.8pp stop-out (22–28% relative): Being shut out of a required course raises the probability of enrolling in no classes at all that term by 2.3–2.8 percentage points — a 22–28% relative increase over a roughly 10% baseline stop-out rate. [Source: Robles et al., 'The effect of course shutouts on community college students,' ScienceDirect]
- +5.41pp persistence (+7.39pp new students): Scheduling tools that reduce conflicts and increase course availability raised persistence by an average of 5.41 percentage points (up to 7.39 points for new students). [Source: Completion-research review of scheduling-technology studies (Community College Daily / SAGE persistence study set)]
- Odessa: +12pp retention / +13% credentials; Kilgore: 26%→33%: Eight-week terms and standardized scheduling blocks improved outcomes: Odessa College in-class retention +12 percentage points and credentials awarded +13%; Kilgore College 150% graduation rate 26%→33%. [Source: Odessa College, Kilgore College; reported via Community College Daily]
